faqs.question: How much does carpenter ant control cost in Queens Village?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ant control in Queens Village typically ranges from $350 to $2,000, depending on the size of your property, severity of the infestation, and number of colonies present. A standard single-family home treatment usually costs $350-$750, while larger properties or severe infestations requiring multiple treatments and structural repairs may reach $2,000. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any work.

faqs.question: How can I tell if I have carpenter ants versus regular ants in my Queens Village home?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ants are significantly larger than common household ants (1/4 to 1/2 inch long) and are typically black or dark brown. You may notice piles of wood shavings (frass) near wooden structures, hear faint rustling sounds inside walls, or see winged ants during spring swarming season. Unlike termites, carpenter ants don't eat wood—they excavate it to build nests, leaving smooth galleries. Our technicians can accurately identify the species during your free inspection.

faqs.question: How quickly can you provide carpenter ant service in Queens Village?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day service to Queens Village in most cases. Because carpenter ants can cause progressive structural damage, we prioritize rapid response times. After your initial call, we can typically schedule an inspection within 24 hours and begin treatment immediately upon approval. Most treatments are completed in one visit, with follow-up monitoring scheduled as needed.

faqs.question: Will carpenter ants damage my Queens Village home?
faqs.answer: Yes, carpenter ants can cause significant structural damage over time by excavating wood to create nesting galleries. They're particularly attracted to moisture-damaged wood in areas like window frames, roof eaves, porches, and basement joists—common trouble spots in Queens Village's older homes. While damage occurs more slowly than with termites, a mature carpenter ant colony can compromise wooden structures. Early detection and professional treatment are essential to preventing costly repairs.
